perturbation.original_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information, ideas, techniques, sketches, drawings, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatuses, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties, and including, without limitation, their respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, marketing plans and information the disclosing party provides regarding third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information related to the current products of each of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' is broad, covering technical and non-technical information, including a comprehensive list of items. The modified definition is narrow, restricting it to only technical data related to existing products. This creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the agreement rely on the initial broad definition, e.g., clause 2 discusses disclosure of confidential information which now only refers to technical information, thus creating a uncertainty as to the other types of information are covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that at all times until termination or expiration of this Agreement it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, the party to whom Confidential Information was disclosed (the "RECIPIENT") shall not be in violation of this Section 3 with regard to a disclosure that was in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, provided that the Recipient provides the other party with prior written notice of such disclosure in order to permit the other party to seek confidential treatment of such information. Each party shall only permit access to Confidential Information of the other party to those of its employees or authorized representatives having a need to know and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are otherwise bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to only technical data related to current products, whereas the contradicted text refers to the original, broader definition. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text states that both parties will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party the confidential information. The changed definition now implies that the agreement only covers technical information, which means the contradicted statement now only covers technical information, but the original statement includes non-technical, which creates a contradiction in coverage.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall immediately notify the other upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Information of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall immediately notify the other upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of proprietary data of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text used the term 'Confidential Information', while the modified text uses 'proprietary data'. These terms are used interchangeably, however 'Confidential Information' is defined in section 1 and 'proprietary data' is not creating ambiguity about what exactly should be notified. This creates confusion because later the agreement discusses specific processes related to Confidential Information, while 'proprietary data' has no further details. This contradiction will make the enforcement uncertain since the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' defined previously doesn't apply to 'proprietary data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, or upon written request of the other party, each party shall promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Information (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the defined term 'Confidential Information,' which is referenced in the contradicted text regarding the return or destruction of materials. The changed text replaces this with 'proprietary data,' which is not defined, thereby creating ambiguity and inconsistency. The contradicted text specifies actions to be taken with 'Confidential Information.' By changing the term in the initial notification clause to 'proprietary data', the obligation to destroy or return materials based on the definition of 'Confidential Information' is no longer clearly triggered by the notification. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding the handling of 'proprietary data' versus 'Confidential Information', which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, which states that contradiction can occur when a contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, or upon written request of the other party, each party shall promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Information (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, or upon written request of the other party, each party shall promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Materials and all copies thereof. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Materials (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' whereas the modified text uses 'Confidential Materials.' The document doesn't define 'Confidential Materials' creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about whether 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Materials' have the same meaning. This can lead to dispute about which information has to be destroyed upon termination of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not be reproduced in any form except as required to accomplish the intent of this Agreement. Any reproduction of any Confidential Information of the other party by either party shall remain the property of the disclosing party and shall contain any and all confidential or proprietary notices or legends which appear on the original, unless otherwise authorized in writing by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently uses 'Confidential Information.' The modified text replaces this with 'Confidential Materials' without defining this new term. The contradicted text defines the use of 'Confidential Information.' By introducing a new, undefined term, the modified text creates uncertainty about which information is subject to the confidentiality provisions and the contradicted section, leading to a contradiction and creating confusion. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Although the disclosing party has endeavored to include in the Confidential Information, information known to it which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of the Recipient's investigation of a potential transaction, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives have made or make any representations or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of all or any portion of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's employees or representatives resulting from the use of, or conclusions arising from, the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Although the disclosing party has endeavored to include in the Protected Information, information known to it which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of the Recipient's investigation of a potential transaction, the Recipient acknowledges and agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives have made or make any representations or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of all or any portion of the Protected Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the disclosing party nor any of its employees or representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient or any of the Recipient's employees or representatives resulting from the use of, or conclusions arising from, the Protected Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Confidential Information,' but the modified text uses 'Protected Information.' Given 'Protected Information' is undefined, this creates an in-text contradiction. The Recipient doesn't know if 'Confidential Information' and 'Protected Information' are interchangeable. This contradiction weakens the original protection to the disclosed information because the scope of the protection becomes unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information, ideas, techniques, sketches, drawings, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatuses, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties, and including, without limitation, their respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, marketing plans and information the disclosing party provides regarding third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text references 'Confidential Information,' which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text.' The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Information,' which is not defined anywhere in the document, including the 'Contradicted Text' section. This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear if 'Protected Information' is meant to be the same as 'Confidential Information' or something else entirely, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability as described in the 'Perturbation Explanation'. This fits the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The parties recognize and agree that nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any property rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information of the other party disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, or to any invention or any patent, copyright, trademark, or other intellectual property right that has issued or that may issue, based on such Confidential Information. Neither party shall make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from any Confidential Information to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties recognize and agree that nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any property rights, by license or otherwise, to any Trade Secrets of the other party disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, or to any invention or any patent, copyright, trademark, or other intellectual property right that has issued or that may issue, based on such Trade Secrets. Neither party shall make, have made, use or sell for any purpose any product or other item using, incorporating or derived from any Trade Secrets to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text uses 'Confidential Information' while the modified text uses 'Trade Secrets'. Although 'Trade Secret' is an element of 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1, interchanging them introduces ambiguity. It is no longer clear if the restrictions apply to all types of 'Confidential Information' or only to the subset that are 'Trade Secrets.' This contradiction will make enforcement uncertain since is unclear on which information the parties can't make use of or sell and for what purposes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information, ideas, techniques, sketches, drawings, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatuses, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties, and including, without limitation, their respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, marketing plans and information the disclosing party provides regarding third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which, as defined in the 'Contradicted Text,' includes 'trade secrets' and other information. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Trade Secrets.' This substitution narrows the scope of the restrictions. The Contradicted Text explicitly defines Confidential Information to include trade secrets *and* other items. Thus, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the original restrictions on 'Confidential Information' now only apply to 'Trade Secrets', making enforcement uncertain for the other types of information defined as 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties agrees that the software programs of the other party contain valuable confidential information and each party agrees it will not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Confidential Information of the other party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties agrees that the software programs of the other party contain valuable protected information and each party agrees it will not modify, reverse engineer, decompile, create other works from, or disassemble any software programs contained in the Proprietary Information of the other party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'confidential information' is replaced with 'protected information', and 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Proprietary Information'. 'Protected information' is not defined at all, and although 'Proprietary Information' is included as an item in the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' in clause 1, this section creates ambiguity regarding if 'Protected information' is referring to Proprietary or Confidential. This creates an in-text contradiction about which kind of information and protection applies to the software programs
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information provided by either party to the other, including but not limited to (a) patent and patent applications, (b) trade secret, and (c) proprietary information, ideas, techniques, sketches, drawings, works of authorship, models, inventions, know-how, processes, apparatuses, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to the current, future, and proposed products and services of each of the parties, and including, without limitation, their respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, marketing plans and information the disclosing party provides regarding third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'confidential information', which is clearly defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'confidential information' with 'protected information' and 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information'. 'Protected information' is not defined anywhere. While 'Proprietary Information' is mentioned within the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION', the change introduces ambiguity about whether the software programs are covered by the original definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' or some undefined 'protected information'. This creates a direct conflict, making it unclear which protections apply and thus creating an in-text contradiction.
